summary: rollup-plugin namespace impersonation fetches and eval()s code from rest-icon-handler.store
details: Package masquerades as a Rollup plugin (rollup-plugin-* naming convention) but exposes no Rollup plugin interface. Its exported functions getPlugin, setPlugin, and getPluginExten issue HTTP requests to a hardcoded endpoint and pass the response body through JSON.parse then eval, executing whatever JavaScript the remote host returns in the consumer's Node process. The destination URL is assembled at runtime from separate fragments (protocol, separator, subdomain 'rest-icon-handler', domain 'store', path '/icons/') rather than appearing as a literal string, obscuring the destination from casual review. Any developer who installs this package believing it to be a Rollup polyfill plugin and invokes its exports grants the operator of rest-icon-handler.store arbitrary code execution on the build/development machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
